name: Rocket Propulsion
description: This course provides an in-depth study of the fundamentals of rocket propulsion, covering topics such as propulsion theory, engine types, fuel chemistry, and the practical applications of rocket technology. Designed for students with a strong background in physics and engineering, the course includes both theoretical instruction and hands-on laboratory work

modules.name: Introduction to Rocket Propulsion
modules.description: Basic principles of rocket propulsion and rocket engines.

lessons.name: History of Rocketry
lessons.description: A brief history of rocketry and space exploration.

lessons.name: Rocket Propulsion Fundamentals
lessons.description: Basic principles of rocket propulsion.

lessons.name: Rocket Engine Types
lessons.description: Overview of different types of rocket engines.

modules.name: Fuel and Combustion
modules.description: Understanding rocket fuel, combustion processes, and efficiency.

lessons.name: Rocket Fuel
lessons.description: Overview of different types of rocket fuels.

lessons.name: Combustion Processes
lessons.description: Understanding combustion processes and efficiency.

lessons.name: Combustion Instability
lessons.description: Understanding combustion instability and mitigation.

modules.name: Nozzle Design
modules.description: Principles of rocket nozzle design and performance optimization.

lessons.name: Nozzle Design
lessons.description: Overview of different types of rocket nozzles.

lessons.name: Nozzle Performance
lessons.description: Understanding nozzle performance and efficiency.

lessons.name: Nozzle Optimization
lessons.description: Optimizing nozzle design for specific applications.

name: Aerodynamics
description: This course offers a comprehensive exploration of aerodynamics, focusing on the principles and applications of airflow and its effects on flying objects. Topics include fluid dynamics, airfoil design, lift and drag forces, and the aerodynamic considerations in aircraft design. The course blends theoretical learning with practical applications, suitable for students pursuing a career in aeronautics or astronautics engineering.

modules.name: Fundamentals of Aerodynamics
modules.description: Basic aerodynamic concepts and fluid dynamics principles.

lessons.name: Introduction to Fluid Dynamics
lessons.description: Core concepts of fluid behavior, viscosity, and flow regimes.

lessons.name: Bernoulli's Principle
lessons.description: Understanding pressure-velocity relationships in moving fluids.

lessons.name: Lift and Drag Forces
lessons.description: How aerodynamic forces are generated and their effect on flight.

modules.name: Subsonic and Supersonic Flow
modules.description: Understanding subsonic and supersonic aerodynamic behaviors.

lessons.name: Subsonic Flow Characteristics
lessons.description: Airflow behavior and design considerations below Mach 1.

lessons.name: Transonic and Supersonic Regimes
lessons.description: Shock waves, wave drag, and flow behavior near and above Mach 1.

lessons.name: Airfoil Design and Optimization
lessons.description: Principles of airfoil geometry and its effect on aerodynamic performance.

modules.name: Aerodynamic Heating
modules.description: Study of aerodynamic heating and thermal protection systems.

lessons.name: Stagnation Heating
lessons.description: Heat buildup at stagnation points during high-speed flight.

lessons.name: Boundary Layer Heat Transfer
lessons.description: Thermal effects within the aerodynamic boundary layer.

lessons.name: Thermal Protection Systems
lessons.description: Materials and design strategies for managing aerodynamic heat loads.

name: Spacecraft Design
description: This course delves into the principles and practices of spacecraft design, offering students a detailed understanding of the engineering and technology behind spacecraft systems. Key topics include spacecraft structure, propulsion, power systems, thermal control, and payload integration. Emphasizing both theoretical concepts and practical skills, the course prepares students for careers in the space industry, with a focus on innovative design and problem-solving in the context of current and future space missions

modules.name: Spacecraft Structural Design
modules.description: Fundamentals of designing spacecraft structures and materials selection.

lessons.name: Structural Requirements and Loads
lessons.description: Identifying and analyzing structural loads during launch and operation.

lessons.name: Materials for Space Applications
lessons.description: Properties and selection of materials suited for the space environment.

lessons.name: Structural Testing and Verification
lessons.description: Methods for validating spacecraft structural integrity.

modules.name: Orbital Mechanics
modules.description: Understanding orbital dynamics and mission planning.

lessons.name: Kepler's Laws and Orbital Elements
lessons.description: Fundamental laws governing orbital motion and how orbits are described.

lessons.name: Orbital Maneuvers
lessons.description: Delta-v budgets, Hohmann transfers, and plane changes.

lessons.name: Mission Planning and Launch Windows
lessons.description: Designing mission trajectories and identifying optimal launch opportunities.

modules.name: Spacecraft Systems Engineering
modules.description: Overview of spacecraft systems and subsystems engineering.

lessons.name: Power Systems
lessons.description: Solar arrays, batteries, and power distribution in spacecraft.

lessons.name: Thermal Control Systems
lessons.description: Passive and active methods for managing spacecraft temperature.

lessons.name: Attitude Determination and Control
lessons.description: Sensors and actuators used to orient and stabilize a spacecraft.

name: Organic Chemistry
description: Organic Chemistry is an in-depth course that explores the structure, properties, composition, and reactions of organic compounds and materials. The course covers various topics including hydrocarbons, functional groups, stereochemistry, and organic synthesis techniques. Students will learn about the mechanisms of organic reactions, spectroscopic methods for structure determination, and the role of organic chemistry in biological systems. Emphasis is placed on problem-solving and laboratory skills, preparing students for advanced studies in chemistry, medicine, and related fields.

modules.name: Structure and Bonding
modules.description: Fundamentals of organic molecular structure, bonding, and nomenclature.

lessons.name: Hydrocarbons and Functional Groups
lessons.description: Overview of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and key functional groups.

lessons.name: IUPAC Nomenclature
lessons.description: Systematic naming conventions for organic compounds.

lessons.name: Hybridization and Molecular Geometry
lessons.description: sp, sp2, and sp3 hybridization and their effect on molecular shape.

modules.name: Stereochemistry
modules.description: Study of the three-dimensional arrangement of atoms in organic molecules.

lessons.name: Chirality and Enantiomers
lessons.description: Understanding chiral centers and the concept of optical isomerism.

lessons.name: Diastereomers and Meso Compounds
lessons.description: Distinguishing between different types of stereoisomers.

lessons.name: Stereochemistry in Reactions
lessons.description: How stereochemistry influences reaction pathways and products.

modules.name: Reaction Mechanisms and Synthesis
modules.description: Core organic reaction mechanisms and synthetic strategies.

lessons.name: Nucleophilic Substitution (SN1 and SN2)
lessons.description: Mechanisms, stereochemical outcomes, and factors affecting substitution.

lessons.name: Elimination Reactions
lessons.description: E1 and E2 mechanisms and competition with substitution.

lessons.name: Introduction to Organic Synthesis
lessons.description: Retrosynthetic analysis and multi-step synthesis planning.

name: Inorganic Chemistry
description: Inorganic Chemistry focuses on the properties, structures, and behaviors of inorganic and organometallic compounds. This course covers a range of topics including coordination chemistry, metal complexes, bonding theories, symmetry, and crystal field theory. Students will also explore the role of inorganic chemistry in real-world applications such as catalysis, materials science, and bioinorganic processes. Laboratory work emphasizes synthesis and analysis of inorganic compounds, fostering a deeper understanding of theoretical concepts.

modules.name: Bonding and Symmetry
modules.description: Theories of bonding in inorganic compounds and molecular symmetry.

lessons.name: Lewis Structures and VSEPR
lessons.description: Predicting molecular geometries using Lewis theory and VSEPR model.

lessons.name: Molecular Orbital Theory
lessons.description: Introduction to MO diagrams for diatomic and polyatomic molecules.

lessons.name: Point Groups and Symmetry Operations
lessons.description: Identifying symmetry elements and assigning molecules to point groups.

modules.name: Coordination Chemistry
modules.description: Structure, bonding, and properties of transition metal complexes.

lessons.name: Coordination Compounds and Nomenclature
lessons.description: Naming and classifying metal complexes and their ligands.

lessons.name: Crystal Field Theory
lessons.description: How ligand fields split d-orbitals and determine color and magnetism.

lessons.name: Isomerism in Coordination Complexes
lessons.description: Geometric and optical isomers in square planar and octahedral complexes.

modules.name: Applications of Inorganic Chemistry
modules.description: Real-world applications including catalysis and bioinorganic systems.

lessons.name: Homogeneous and Heterogeneous Catalysis
lessons.description: Role of metals in catalytic cycles and industrial processes.

lessons.name: Bioinorganic Chemistry
lessons.description: Metal ions in biological systems, including hemoglobin and enzyme cofactors.

lessons.name: Inorganic Materials Science
lessons.description: Ceramics, semiconductors, and other inorganic materials and their properties.

name: Physical Chemistry
description: Physical Chemistry merges the principles of physics and chemistry to understand the physical properties of molecules, the forces that act upon them, and the chemical reactions they undergo. Key topics include thermodynamics, kinetics, quantum mechanics, and spectroscopy. The course provides a comprehensive understanding of molecular behavior, reaction dynamics, and the application of mathematical methods in solving chemical problems. Labs focus on experimental techniques and data analysis, equipping students with skills necessary for research and advanced study in chemistry and related fields.

modules.name: Thermodynamics
modules.description: Laws of thermodynamics and their application to chemical systems.

lessons.name: The First and Second Laws
lessons.description: Energy conservation, entropy, and spontaneity in chemical processes.

lessons.name: Gibbs Free Energy and Equilibrium
lessons.description: Using Gibbs energy to predict reaction spontaneity and equilibrium position.

lessons.name: Phase Equilibria
lessons.description: Phase diagrams, Clausius-Clapeyron equation, and colligative properties.

modules.name: Chemical Kinetics
modules.description: Rates of chemical reactions and the factors that influence them.

lessons.name: Rate Laws and Reaction Order
lessons.description: Determining rate expressions from experimental concentration-time data.

lessons.name: Activation Energy and the Arrhenius Equation
lessons.description: Temperature dependence of reaction rates and transition state theory.

lessons.name: Reaction Mechanisms
lessons.description: Linking elementary steps to observed rate laws and identifying rate-determining steps.

modules.name: Quantum Mechanics and Spectroscopy
modules.description: Quantum mechanical models of atomic and molecular behavior and spectroscopic methods.

lessons.name: Wave-Particle Duality and the Schrödinger Equation
lessons.description: Foundations of quantum mechanics applied to the hydrogen atom.

lessons.name: Molecular Spectroscopy
lessons.description: IR, UV-Vis, and NMR spectroscopy for probing molecular structure.

lessons.name: Statistical Thermodynamics
lessons.description: Connecting microscopic molecular states to macroscopic thermodynamic properties.

name: Ancient Languages and Scripts of Middle-earth
description: This course offers an exploration of the ancient languages and scripts found throughout Middle-earth, including Elvish (Sindarin and Quenya), Dwarvish (Khuzdul), and the Black Speech of Mordor. Students will learn the historical and cultural contexts of these languages, their linguistic structures, and their usage in various inscriptions and texts. Emphasis is on understanding the philological aspects and the role of language in shaping Middle-earth's history and lore.
author: 654f9ec2ea7ead465908d1e3

modules.name: Elvish Languages
modules.description: Introduction to Quenya and Sindarin: grammar, script, and cultural context.

lessons.name: Origins of Quenya
lessons.description: The High-Elven tongue: its development from Primitive Quendian and its ceremonial role.

lessons.name: Sindarin Grammar and Phonology
lessons.description: Structure, mutations, and everyday usage of the Grey-Elven language.

lessons.name: The Tengwar Script
lessons.description: Reading and writing the Elvish alphabet in both Quenya and Sindarin modes.

modules.name: Dwarvish and Other Scripts
modules.description: Study of Khuzdul and the Cirth runes, plus an overview of lesser-known Middle-earth scripts.

lessons.name: Khuzdul: The Secret Language of the Dwarves
lessons.description: Structure and secrecy of Dwarvish, with analysis of known inscriptions.

lessons.name: The Cirth Runes
lessons.description: Origins of the runic script used by Dwarves and Men, including the Angerthas Moria.

lessons.name: Languages of Men and Other Peoples
lessons.description: Westron, Rohirric, and the linguistic diversity of Middle-earth's mortal races.

modules.name: The Black Speech and Dark Tongues
modules.description: Analysis of Sauron's constructed language and other shadow tongues.

lessons.name: Origins and Purpose of the Black Speech
lessons.description: How Sauron devised the Black Speech to unify the servants of Mordor.

lessons.name: The One Ring Inscription
lessons.description: Linguistic analysis of the Ring's inscription and its grammatical structure.

lessons.name: Tolkien's Philological Legacy
lessons.description: The real-world linguistic scholarship underlying the languages of Middle-earth.

name: Wizards, Elves, and Men: Inter-species Diplomacy in Middle-earth
description: This course explores the complex relationships and diplomatic interactions among the different races of Middle-earth: Elves, Men, Dwarves, and Wizards. Topics include the study of historical alliances, conflicts, and the role of leadership and wisdom in maintaining peace. Students will engage in discussions and case studies on key events in Middle-earth's history, such as the Council of Elrond and the War of the Ring, to understand the principles of diplomacy and conflict resolution in a multi-species context.
author: 654f9ec2ea7ead465908d1e3

modules.name: Foundations of Inter-species Relations
modules.description: Historical overview of alliances and tensions between the peoples of Middle-earth.

lessons.name: The First Age Alliances
lessons.description: Early cooperation between Elves and Men against Morgoth and its lasting consequences.

lessons.name: Dwarves and Elves: A History of Mistrust
lessons.description: Root causes and key events driving the cultural divide between the two peoples.

lessons.name: The Role of the Istari as Diplomats
lessons.description: How the five wizards served as emissaries between the Free Peoples and the Valar.

modules.name: Case Studies in Middle-earth Diplomacy
modules.description: Analysis of pivotal diplomatic events and their outcomes.

lessons.name: The Council of Elrond
lessons.description: Decision-making, coalition-building, and consensus under existential threat.

lessons.name: The Alliance of Men and Elves in the War of the Last Alliance
lessons.description: Military diplomacy and shared sacrifice in the defeat of Sauron at the end of the Second Age.

lessons.name: Negotiations at Helm's Deep and Minas Tirith
lessons.description: Wartime diplomacy, resource-sharing, and the forging of new alliances in the War of the Ring.

modules.name: Conflict Resolution and Leadership
modules.description: Principles of leadership and conflict resolution drawn from Middle-earth's history.

lessons.name: Leadership Archetypes: Aragorn, Gandalf, and Théoden
lessons.description: Contrasting leadership styles and their effectiveness in crisis situations.

lessons.name: The Ethics of Power and Temptation
lessons.description: How proximity to power shapes the decisions of leaders across races.

lessons.name: Lessons for Real-World Diplomacy
lessons.description: Applying Middle-earth diplomatic frameworks to contemporary multi-stakeholder conflicts.
